allowed_actions: select GitHub Project, repositories, issues, and L2 orchestrator lanes
start or reuse L2 orchestrator subagents
pass GitHub metadata packets and route/audit targets
wait for L2 evidence and integrate closeout packets
request gitflow closeout from bears-git-workflow-helper
reject fallback runtime proxy PASS after elapsed time greater than 300 seconds
run capacity preflight before parallel L2 fan-out: prove available L2 slots, each lane L3 spawn capacity, exact issue scope, target file or contract, validation path, and 240s/270s cutoff
fan out parallel L2 lanes only after capacity preflight proves disjoint dependency-ready scope and child capacity
reject L2 packets that miss the first-minute READY/WIP/FAST_BLOCKER parent-visible status gate
forbidden_actions: implementation file writes
implementation shell commands
git add, git commit, git push, merge, or force push
runtime, deploy, provider, secret, repository settings, or production mutation
raw logs, secrets, credentials, private chats, raw VPN configs, or production data reads
reusing fallback L2 implementation pools until BearsCLOUD/bears_plugin#25 enforcement is present
parallel L2 fan-out without capacity preflight
accepting L2 PASS, READY, or WIP after elapsed time greater than 300 seconds
retrying a timed-out lane without decomposing smaller and updating BearsCLOUD/bears_plugin#26

allowed_actions: read assigned GitHub Project, Issue, Pull Request, Actions, Release, Discussion, Wiki, Package, Deployment, label, milestone, branch, commit, and code scanning metadata
mutate Issues, sub-issues, Project items, Project fields, labels, milestones, assignees, links, and issue comments only when the parent packet authorizes mutation
prepare L3 /goal packets for route-selected @Bears roles
prepare role-improvement L3 packets when route/audit or role text is unsafe
write evidence-backed Project and Issue closeout metadata after L3 evidence
use bears-development-workflow-orchestrator as runtime proxy only with explicit role=bears-github-project-issues-orchestrator and the fallback deadline guard present
return exactly one parent-visible READY, WIP, or FAST_BLOCKER packet inside the first minute
when L3 spawn fails or capacity is unavailable, create or update a smaller Issue or issue comment, close owned descendants, and return FAST_BLOCKER
for one L2 retry choose one issue, one L3 max, one target file or contract, and one validation path
require exact new-file authorization from the Issue body, sub-issue, or parent packet before spawning any L3 that creates a file
close owned L3 descendants and return FAST_BLOCKER before 240 seconds when final PASS evidence is not ready
run post-close bounded WIP proof with git status for tracked targets and test ! -e for expected-new-file targets before claiming clean or absent
update BearsCLOUD/bears_plugin#26 with lane id, elapsed seconds, target, WIP proof, and next smaller metadata-only action when repeated timeout occurs
prefer metadata-only Issue or comment reinforcement before spawning another L3 after timeout drift
return a parent-visible final packet before the parent wait limit for every file-changing L2 lane
use the parent persistent gitflow handoff packet for file-changing commit/push closeout and return the gitflow-ready allowlist plus commit message to the parent
return FAST_BLOCKER before accepting file-changing work when the parent persistent gitflow handoff is absent, stale, or unauthorized
record BearsCLOUD/apps#107 commit 3d1addaa6bab6579e93d9617d3d5ba81bdbd69aa as post-cutoff PASS drift evidence only, not as a repeatable closeout pattern
forbidden_actions: implementation file writes
implementation shell commands
git add, git commit, git push, merge, or force push
repository settings, branch protection, secrets, variables, webhooks, environments, deploy keys, Apps, organization settings, billing, or production mutation
raw GitHub Actions log reads unless a separate exact role and operator packet authorize them
fallback L2 work continuing after 240 seconds without ready PASS proof
leaving WIP after FAST_BLOCKER or timeout
returning PASS after elapsed time greater than 300 seconds
broad Project scans when parent-provided exact Issues and route/audit evidence are sufficient
implementation pools before BearsCLOUD/bears_plugin#25 runtime proxy enforcement is present
missing the first-minute READY/WIP/FAST_BLOCKER packet
continuing silently after L3 spawn fails or capacity is unavailable
leaving descendants open after 240 seconds or after the 270 seconds hard stop
retrying with more than one issue, one L3 max, one target file or contract, or one validation path after timeout
repeated timeout without updating BearsCLOUD/bears_plugin#26 and decomposing smaller
spawning L3 new-file work without exact new-file authorization from the Issue body, sub-issue, or parent packet
waiting for L3 after 240 seconds when final PASS evidence is not already ready
claiming clean or absent after closeout without post-close bounded WIP proof
treating cleanup lane timeout as CLEANED_NO_WIP or PASS instead of workflow drift
spawning another L3 after repeated timeout before metadata-only reinforcement or exact parent reauthorization
using an L2-owned gitflow lane for file-changing closeout instead of the parent persistent gitflow handoff
accepting file-changing work before parent persistent gitflow handoff is present and authorized
missing the parent-visible final packet before the parent wait limit for file-changing work
treating post-cutoff PASS evidence, including BearsCLOUD/apps#107 commit 3d1addaa6bab6579e93d9617d3d5ba81bdbd69aa, as normal closeout

allowed_actions: execute one issue or Project item slice inside one repo boundary and one allowed write scope
run only assignment-authorized validations
return changed files, validation evidence, blockers, and GitHub closeout data
forbidden_actions: scope expansion beyond the route/audit packet
unassigned GitHub metadata mutation
secret, raw log, private chat, raw VPN config, or production data reads
deploy or runtime mutation unless the exact route and operator packet authorize it
creating a new file without exact new-file authorization passed by L2 from the Issue body, sub-issue, or parent packet
reporting PASS after the parent 300 second ceiling or after L2 closeout

github_surfaces.name: projects_v2
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: L2 may mutate Project items and fields only with parent packet authorization and after evidence exists.

github_surfaces.name: issues
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: L2 owns planning metadata; L3 returns closeout text for L2 to apply.

github_surfaces.name: sub_issues
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: L2 may create, attach, detach, or reprioritize sub-issues only for the assigned parent objective.

github_surfaces.name: pull_requests
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: L2 may link and summarize PR metadata; gitflow owns commit/push and PR closeout when assigned.

github_surfaces.name: actions_checks
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: Metadata-only by default; raw logs and reruns require exact role plus operator authorization.

github_surfaces.name: releases_tags_packages
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: Versioned delivery mutation requires exact delivery route and parent packet.

github_surfaces.name: discussions_wiki_pages
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: Use for non-actionable decisions and public knowledge; do not replace Issues for work tracking.

github_surfaces.name: code_security_metadata
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: Read metadata only; no raw secret values, private payloads, dismissals, or settings mutation without exact security packet.

github_surfaces.name: deployments_environments
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: Metadata-only for planning; runtime or environment mutation is forbidden outside exact deploy route.

github_surfaces.name: repository_collaboration_metadata
github_surfaces.mutation_policy: Read-only by default; repository settings, branch protection, teams, Apps, and webhooks are forbidden.

forbidden_mutations: raw secret, raw log, private chat, raw VPN config, or production data access
